subject: Sports
title: Leadership Styles in Sports
description: A quick win: understand Leadership Styles in Sports.

hook.text: Some captains lead by example. Others lead with words. The best know when to switch between the two.

buildup.text: Research identifies task-oriented leaders (focused on strategy) and relationship-oriented leaders (focused on morale).

discovery.text: The most effective sports leaders adapt their style to the situation—demanding during competition, supportive during setbacks.

twist.text: Michael Jordan's leadership was brutal—he bullied teammates into greatness. It worked, but it traumatized some.

climax.text: Tim Duncan led quietly by example. Jordan led loudly by force. Both won five-plus championships.

punchline.text: There's no single way to lead. The best leaders match the moment.

quiz.question: What makes the most effective sports leaders?
options: They adapt their leadership style to the situation
They are always the most talented player on the team
They focus exclusively on motivation and morale
